q: What is the reactive intermediate derived from tyrosine that is proposed to undergo intramolecular cyclization by adding to the functional group which was attempted to be incorporated into an acyl aziridine moiety in the synthetic strategy described?

path.q: Which reactive intermediate derived from tyrosine is proposed to undergo intramolecular cyclization by adding to the histidine imidazole?

path.text:
of a nonepimerizable surrogate 
for the primary amide. For construction of the bbranched tyrosine 
moiety of the eastern fragment 4, a broad range of strategies were 
evaluated (Figure 2, strategies BD). Linkages of this type are 
extremely rare and notoriously difficult to obtain synthetically. Indeed, no successful strategy has been uncovered to date.1920 
Attempted nucleophilic substitution reactions under either neutral, 
anionic or halogenideAgfacilitated conditions led consistently to 
either formation of the corresponding unsaturated dehydroamino 
acid or decomposition of the substrates (Figure 2b, strategy B, see 
SI). Attempts to utilize addition of the histidine imidazole to an acyl 
aziridine moiety were hampered by the electronic nature of the 
acyclic twisted amide such obtained (Figure 2b, strategy C).
path.q: Which functional group was attempted to be added to an acyl aziridine moiety in the synthetic strategy described?

q: In the synthesis of pyrazole 2, bromide 3 is first treated with the reagent that activates it for further reaction. Which reagent is introduced next to complete this transformation?

path.text:
Both routes rely on the modification of the already installed 
functional groups (a) oxidation of the thiomethyl group;4 and (b) 
metalmediated crosscoupling of halidesboronates with sodium 
sulfinate5,6 or sulphur dioxidemethyl iodide (Figure 1).7,8,9,10 ,11 
Here, we present a principally novel approach to methyl 
sulfones that relies on the 1,3heterocycle disconnection logic 
with the newly developed reagent 1 (Figure 1). Results and discussion 
Background. In our daily practice, we often use two standard 
approaches to methyl sulfones described above (Figure 1). Recently, we received a request from a pharmaceutical 
company for a synthesis of pyrazole 2 (Scheme 1), which was 
described in a patent before.12 In that method, bromide 3 was 
treated first with nBuLi followed by the addition of (MeS)2.
path.q: In the synthesis of pyrazole 2, after bromide 3 is treated with nBuLi, which reagent is subsequently added?
